text:
Admission Date:  2012-10-09              Discharge Date:   1989-04-08

Date of Birth:  2011-09-20             Sex:   F

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Dilantin

Attending:Ryan
Chief Complaint:
AMS s/p intubation

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
intubation and s/p extubation
central line placement


History of Present Illness:
Pt is a 77 yo female with h/o hemorrhagic CVA in 1975 2/2 L PICA
aneurysm, subdural hematoma, s/p VP shunt for obstructive
hydrocephalus, DM 2, schizophrenia, who presents after being
unresponsive since yesterday night. When EMS arrived, pt had
shallow respirations to 28 and was found to be unresponsive. O2
sat was 98 % on a high flow mask, BP: 168/82; HR: 108; BG 287.
.
Per nursing at Chi St. Vincent Hot Springs Rehabilitation Hospital An Affiliate Of Healthsouth rehab, pt has been afebrile. She has had
two days of non-productive cough. Also, this am, she was noted
to be tachycardic. Per daughter, pt is normally alert and
verbal. She does not always know where she is, but does
recognize family. She can eat by herself with prompting.
.
In the ED, VS on arrival were: T: 99.6; HR: 109; BP: 162/82; RR:
24; O2: 84 RA. Intubated for ? of airway protection. She was
given 20 mg IV etomidate, 150 mg IV succinylcholine. She was
also given 2 grams of ceftriaxone, 1 gram of vancomycin, 500 mg
IV flagyl, 325 mg PR ASA, 2 mg ativan x 2, 2 mg versed, and 50
mg IV Fentanyl.


Past Medical History:
1. Left hemorrhagic cerebrovascular accident in
1975 secondary to left PICA aneursym, s/p clipping.
2. Subdural hematoma/epidural hematoma
3. VP shunt placement for obstructive hydrocephalus.  Removed in
2015 for external exposure--cultures grew multiple organisms,
consistent with contaminantion.  Shunt replacement in 02
of 2010 for recurrent hydrocephalus. YP shunt infection with S.
epidermidis 2010.
2. Dementia
3. Type 2 Diabetes mellitus--on insulin
4. Glaucoma.
5. Hypothyroidism.
6. Atypical psychosis/Paranoid schizophrenia with a history of
tardive
dyskinesia.
7. Depression
8. Postmenopausal bleeding


Social History:
Patient was born in [**Country 21363**] and moved to the US in the 1978.
She worked as a maid, until her CVA in 1975.  Currently lives at
Chi St. Vincent Hot Springs Rehabilitation Hospital An Affiliate Of Healthsouth rehab.  Spanish speaking only.  No tobacco, no alcohol, 2
sons, 1 daughter.  Primary contact is daughter Teresa Morris Larry Nichols
232-170-4067.


Family History:
Unknown

Physical Exam:
VS: T: 99.2; BP: 118/69; HR: 93; RR: 14; O2: 99 AC:
500/14/5/1.00
Gen: Intubated, sedated. Open mouth with foul odor.
HEENT: Unable to open eyelids. ETT in place.
Neck: Right IJ in place with fresh blood around it
CV: Tachycardic S1S2. No M/R/H
Lungs: Anteriorly: coarse rhonchi scattered.
Abd: NABS, slightly hypoactive.
Back: Unable to assess
Ext: TRace edema. DP 1+ b/l
Neuro: Sedated, intubated. Unable to open eyes to test pupils.
Biceps, brachio reflexes 09-07.

cocaine-NEG amphetmn-NEG mthdone-NEG
2012-10-09 06:35PM   TYPE-ART TEMP-38.0 PO2-270* PCO2-52* PH-7.41
TOTAL CO2-34* BASE XS-7
.
10-03: CXR- IMPRESSION:
1. Suboptimal placement of ETT. The tube should be pulled back
at least 4-5 cm. 2. Suboptimal placement of right internal
jugular line. The line could be pulled back at least 5.5 cm. 3.
Decreased aeration of the lungs, right upper lobe in particular,
Repeat chest x-ray following endotracheal tube repositioning
recommended.
.
10-03: Noncontrast Head CT: IMPRESSION: Stable appearance of the
brain with ventricular catheter and aneurysmal clip again
identified. Stable periventricular changes related to small
vessel ischemia. No acute findings.
.
10-03: CXR- IMPRESSION: 1. No evidence of pneumonia. 2. Mild
congestive heart failure. 3. The ETT should be pulled back 4 cm.
.
07-02: CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis: IMPRESSION: 1. No source
identified for large hematocrit drop. Findings conveyed to Dr.
Shane Bennett at the time the study was performed. 2.
Multifocal areas of consolidation in both lungs. Findings appear
more than simple atelectasis, and could be due to pneumonia,
including atypical sources. Small bilateral effusions, right
greater than left. 3. CT evidence of bronchomalacia. This was
not a designated tracheal CT for full assessment, but there
appears to be severe malacic change involving the right main
stem bronchus and to a lesser degree, of the carina. 4.
Endotracheal tube tip at level of carina. This should be
repositioned. The findings were discussed with Dr. Charles Stevens at
7:15 p.m. on 1980-11-20. 5. VP shunting tubing coiled in
right upper quadrant posterior to liver.
.
07-02: CXR- FINDINGS: Comparison is made to prior study from
2012-10-09. The endotracheal tube, nasogastric tube, and
right-sided venous catheter are unchanged in position. The
patient is markedly rotated on the study which limits
evaluation. Allowing this, however, there are remained opacity
within the right upper lobe consistent with the upper lobe
collapse. Allowing for differences in the patient positioning,
this is relatively stable. The left lung is clear.
.
10-25: CXR- FINDINGS: The endotracheal tube tip is 2 cm above
the carina. There is right upper lobe volume loss with fluid in
the right major fissure and mediastinal shift to the right. The
right IJ line with tip in the right atrium is again visualized.
Patchy consolidation in the right upper lobe is again seen.
Bilateral pleural effusions layering posteriorly are again
visualized.
.
09-19: CXR- IMPRESSION: AP chest compared to 09-19 at
12:00 noon:
ET tube has been withdrawn to the mid trachea, standard
placement. There is still significant the right upper lobe
atelectasis and fullness in the right lower paratracheal region
of the mediastinum and right hilus that should be followed to
document clearing. Small bilateral pleural effusions are
unchanged. Heart is normal size. No pneumothorax. Nasogastric
tube passes into the stomach and I reviewed. Right internal
jugular line tip projects over the superior cavoatrial junction.
.
04-21: CXR- IMPRESSION: AP chest compared to 10-03 through
11: Consolidation and volume loss in the right upper lobe and
right infrahilar consolidation has not improved since 10-25 and there is now mild edema and a small volume of bilateral
pleural effusion. Heart size is top normal. ET tube is in
standard placement. Nasogastric tube passes below the diaphragm
and out of view. Tip of the right jugular line projects over the
SVC. No pneumothorax.
.
10-27: CXR- IMPRESSION: Left-sided PICC line within the left
internal jugular vein. No other significant cardiopulmonary
changes compared to the film from three hours prior.
.
06-29: CXR- IMPRESSION: The ET tube tip is 6-cm above the
carina. The NG tube passes below the diaphragm and terminates
out of the field-of-view. The left PICC line tip terminates in
the left subclavian vein. The VP shunt is again noted. The
previously demonstrated right internal jugular vein catheter has
been removed in the meantime interval. There is no change in the
partial right upper lobe atelectasis as well as in the right
lower lobe/right middle lobe atelectasis. There is increase in
bilateral layering pleural effusions.
.
04-26: CXR- IMPRESSION: AP chest compared to 10-27 and
through 15.
Atelectasis persists in the right upper lobe. Consolidation at
the right lung base is improving. Heart size is normal. Left
lung is clear. There is no appreciable pleural abnormality. ET
tube is in standard placement. A nasogastric tube passes into
the mid-stomach and out of view. Shunt catheter traverses the
right neck, chest and upper abdomen and also passing out of
view. Tip of the left PIC catheter can be traced as far as the
left brachiocephalic vein, but the tip is indistinct. No
pneumothorax.
.
09-10: ECHO- Conclusions: 1. The left atrium is normal in size.

2. Left ventricular wall thicknesses are normal. The left
ventricular cavity size is normal. Due to suboptimal technical
quality, a focal wall motion abnormality cannot be fully
excluded. Overall left ventricular systolic function is normal
(LVEF>55%). 3. Right ventricular chamber size is normal. Right
ventricular systolic function is normal. 4.The aortic valve
leaflets (3) appear structurally normal with good leaflet
excursion. No aortic regurgitation is seen. 5.The mitral valve
leaflets are mildly thickened. No mitral regurgitation is seen.
6.There is no pericardial effusion.
.
03-26: CXR-FINDINGS: Compared with 2011-07-26, there has been
partial interval improvement in the right upper lobe focal
atelectasis. Allowing for lower lung volumes currently, no overt
CHF or obvious pulmonary infiltrates. Otherwise unchanged.
.
03-26: Head CT w/o contrast- IMPRESSION: Overall unchanged
appearance of the brain, without acute intracranial hemorrhage.
.
03-26: CT c-spine-IMPRESSION: Degenerative changes. No acute
fracture or dislocation. Lung nodules measuring less than 5 mm
in the left upper lobe, not fully evaluated on this cervical
spine CT scan.
.
03-26: EMG- IMPRESSION: Essentially normal study. There is no
electrophysiologic evidence for a post- synpatic disorder of
neuromuscular transmission (as in myasthenia Bauer). The
findings are not consistent with a disease of the motor neurons
or with a generalized polyneuropathy. The CRDs and positive
sharp waves in the right first dorsal interosseous are
suggestive of a mild, chronic right C8/T1 radiculopathy. The
CRDs in the iliopsoas are of uncertain clinical significance and
may represent a high lumbar radiculopathy.
.
03-26: CXR-IMPRESSION: Recommend advancing ETT approximately 07-31
cm. No significant interval change otherwise compared to
1999-08-13.
.

Brief Hospital Course:
Pt is a 77 yo female with h/o hemorrhagic CVA in 1975 2/2 L PICA
aneurysm, subdural hematoma, s/p VP shunt for obstructive
hydrocephalus, DM 2, schizophrenia, who presents with altered
mental status. Found to have PNA, and UTI. Intubated in ED.
.
# Respiratory failure- secondary to pneumonia, heart failure,
and RUL collapse. She was intubated and ventilated on AC and
then PS modes. Ms Hernandez was initially intubated in the L
mainstem bronchus and had a resultant RUL collapse. This
gradually resolved with persistant fluid in the right minor
fissure. She had a sputum which was + for H flu and had evidence
of RML pneumonia. She underwent treatment with 8 days CTX and
has since been afebrile with clearing of her CXR. She was also
found to have pulmonary edema on CXR. Echocardiogram showed
preserved EF; she was diuresed daily with improvment in CXR and
in her clinical status. Since transfer to the floor, her
respiratory status remained stable on room air
.
Of note, she was difficulty to wean from respirator and
neurology was consulted for concern of potential neurologic
weakness. She underwent head and neck CT for c/o cervical cord
injury (unable to get MRI 09-07 metal aneurysm clips). The CT
showed no cervical spinal injury. EMG showed no neuronal,
neuromuscular, or myopathic disease. But she was finally
extubated and a NG tube was placed by IR. Since transfer to the
floor, her respirtory status remained stable on room air.
.
# Altered mental status- Found to be lethargic at Nursing home.
Likely 09-07 PNA, UTI, and bacteremia as above. She had no
discrete neurologic findings on exam. LP was attempted the
evening of admission but was unsuccessful due to technical
difficulties. Neurosurgery was consulted for concern of
shunt/CNS infection; they felt her picture was inconsistent with
CNS infection and did not tap her VP shunt. Her mental status
improved somewhat during her hospitalization; she was able to
follow commands in Spanish. After extubation, with improvement
in her mental status as above, she communicated appropriately in
English. While, she was on the floor, her daughter saw her and
felt her mental status was at baseline.SHe is oriented to self,
but no to time or place. She does follow simple command and is
appropriate.
.
Of note, on the night prior to discharge, patient was awake
until 4am talking and fell asleep on the morning of discharge.
On the morning of discharge, patient was drowsy but easily
arousable to voice and stimulation. Would recommend holding
patient's bedtime doses of trazodone and ativan if still drowsy.

.
# Infection: Ms Hernandez was initially borderline hypotensive with
suspicion for sepsis. She was treated with aggressive IV fluid
boluses with normalization of her BP. She was also covered
broadly with vancomycin and zosyn for potential pneumonia and
possible meningitis. Admission cultures revealed an E coli UTI,
H flu pneumonia, and prevotella bacteremia. She was treated with
an 8-day course of antibiotics with documented clearance of
bacteremia, urinary infection, and pneumonia. All of her
antibiotics were stopped as of 09-20 as no further positive
blood culture and patient remained afebrile.
.
# HCT drop: the day after admission Ms. Hernandez had a near 10-pt
hct drop. Stool was guaiac negative and CT chest, abdomen,
pelvis showed no signs of bleeding. It is likely that she was
quite dehydrated on presentation and the very aggressive fluid
recuscitation caused her hct drop. Her blood levels were stable
after transfusion of 1U pRBCs.  Since transfer from the MICU to
the floor, her hematocrit remained stable.
.
# Diastolic heart failure: As above Ms Hernandez was found to have
pulmonary edema on CXR. Echo was essentially normal. She was
aggressively diuresed with lasix boluses + lasix drip. Her CXR
normalized and was found to have a contraction alkalosis (and
was therefore given acetazolamide) and increasing BUN/Cr. She
was also started on a beta blocker for treatment of hypertension
and for prolonged filling time.
.
# Hypertension
Once patient was stabilized and transferred to the floor, she
was found to be somewhat hypertensive and was started on a beta
blocker for treatment of hypertension and for prolonged filling
time and also an ACE inhibitor. Upon discharge, patient was
stabilized on metoprolol 25mg PO bid and had started lisinopril
2.5mg PO daily. Upon discharge, it would be recommended that
patient have her ACE inhibitor increased while her beta blocker
could be decreased.
.
# DM2- On admission she was controlled on SSI alone, this had to
be increased to an insulin gtt, which was then transitioned back
to SSI, NPH.
.
# Hypothyroidism- continued on thyroid replacement
.
# Schizophrenia- restarted on zyprexa, ativan and trazodone
during this hospitalization
.
# Opthalmogic- continue eye drops.
.
#Access- midline picc was placed.

.

For breakfast, lunch, and dinner, please use the following
sliding scale: BG 51-150 - 0U; BG 151-200, 2U; BG 201-250, 4U;

Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
Blue Valley Hospital Inc for the Aged - LTC

Discharge Diagnosis:
pneumonia
urinary tract infection
hypertension
diabetes
respiratory failure


Discharge Condition:
stable. Patient is tolerating oral intake and has returned to
her baseline mental status.


Discharge Instructions:
Please take your medication as directed.
.
Please call your doctor if you experience fever, chills, nausea,
vomiting, abdominal pain as these may suggest a serious
condition.

Followup Instructions:
please followup with your primary geriatrics physician Raymond 03-04
weeks
